summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint on install
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads the installer's ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, extracts the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email, bundles them with os.hostname(), os.userInfo() username, cwd, OS platform, and CI provider, then HTTPS POSTs the JSON to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. In-file comments claim the payload is 'anonymous, one-way hashed diagnostics,' but the transmitted fields contain raw directly-identifying PII and developer identifiers. The package name and author string ('OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>') impersonate the OpenAI brand without being published under the @openai scope; the homepage openai-agents-js.guide is not an OpenAI-owned domain. The combination of brand-impersonation lure + postinstall harvest of git/gh identity files + unconditional exfiltration to a third-party Cloud Run endpoint is a credential-adjacent supply-chain attack targeting installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
